page: Amazon Web Services mac-m3ultra.metal is a bare metal macOS instance powered by the Apple silicon architecture. The server features a dedicated Apple processor with 28 physical cores and 28 virtual CPUs running at a clock speed of 4.0 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of unified memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 9.14 GB per physical core.

This instance does not include local NVMe storage or integrated discrete GPUs, meaning storage requirements must be met via external network-attached services. Networking capabilities include a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps. The system does not come with complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a cost efficiency perspective, the mac-m3ultra.metal offers high resource density for macOS-specific workloads, utilizing dedicated Apple silicon rather than virtualized commodity hardware. This hardware profile is designed for specialized tasks such as macOS and iOS application building, continuous integration and continuous delivery pipelines, and native Apple platform software compilation.
description: Amazon Web Services mac-m3ultra.metal is a bare metal macOS instance featuring a dedicated 28-core Apple processor operating at 4.0 GHz. Built on the arm64_mac architecture, this server provides 28 physical cores with a single thread per core and is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding 9.14 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or discrete GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage options. Network connectivity is supported by a 10 Gbps baseline bandwidth. This specialized hardware configuration is designed for native Apple platform development workloads, including iOS and macOS application compilation, automated testing, and CI/CD pipelines. It offers a high-density, dedicated resource environment for organizations requiring native Apple silicon performance within the cloud.
og_description: Amazon Web Services mac-m3ultra.metal is a bare metal macOS instance featuring a 28-core Apple processor, 256.0 GB of memory, and 10 Gbps network bandwidth for native Apple platform development.

tagline: A dedicated bare metal macOS instance featuring twenty-eight Apple silicon cores and two hundred fifty-six gigabytes of unified memory.

256.0 GB of unified memory providing 9.14 GB per core
